title: Urdu ki Panchwin Kitab
gloss: Fifth Urdu Reader for the 5th Class in Girls' Schools, prepared by the Text-Book Revision Committee, Punjab.
copyright: The Punjab Text-Book Committee, Lahore; No. 140, dated the 6th September, 1910.
notes: Completes the girls' reader series (3rd–5th class, regs 1097–1099).

flags.issue: margin digit prints like '14' but sequence (…9, 10, then this) indicates 11

title: Sharh-i-Qanun-i-Haq-i-Shufa' Punjab
gloss: The law of pre-emption—Act No. 2 of 1905—annotated by Shaikh Ghulam Rasul, Superintendent, Rast Guftar Press, Amritsar.
notes: Vernacular legal commentary on the Punjab Pre-emption Act. Four-part pagination. [Previous edition noticed in entry No. 412 on page 67 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1906.]

title: Sharh-i-Qanun-i-Intiqal-i-Arazi, Punjab
gloss: The Punjab Land Alienation Act No. 13 of 1900, annotated.
publisher: Shaikh Ghulam Rasul, Superintendent, Rast Guftar Press
notes: Urdu commentary on the Land Alienation Act 1900 — the central agrarian legislation of colonial Punjab, sold at Re. 1 to a vernacular readership. [Previous edition noticed in entry No. 6 at page 28 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st December, 1909.]

author: Ghulam Ahmad, Babu
title: Purana Kashmiri Kok Shastar
gloss: For subject see entry No. 2 at page 30 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March, 1910.
notes: URDU—MEDICINE serials continue from Q2. Cross-reference: 1910Q1 entry 2, page 30 (Kok Shastar — erotology/sexual medicine).

title: Ilm-o-Amal-i-Fan-i-Jarrahi-i-Aspan, ba taswir
gloss: An Urdu translation of Pease's Equine Surgery, by Lala Prabhu Lal, Translator and Head Clerk, Punjab Veterinary College, Lahore, with 405 illustrations.
notes: Date given only as May 1910. 896 pages, 405 illustrations, Rs. 8 — major veterinary-science translation for the Punjab Veterinary College. Reg 921 here is clear; the Pushto entry (p25) flagged 921/924 is therefore likely 924.
